content.question: Does the current institutional capacity of KLIK-successor bodies, school maintainers, and the teacher workforce (given documented shortages and reported center-periphery tension) allow for successful delivery of a multi-year, participatory reform that requires widespread school-level redesign and expectation management?
content.reduction_path: Conduct an organizational-readiness assessment of KLIK successor structure, school-district implementer capacity, and teacher association engagement levels; identify specific implementation bottlenecks (e.g., administrative bandwidth, professional-development resource gaps, grievance mechanisms); pilot a single high-readiness region with enhanced central support (dedicated reform coordinator, professional-development funding) to establish whether capacity constraints are systemic or addressable through targeted resource reallocation; measure implementation fidelity and staff satisfaction in pilot vs. non-pilot regions.
